{{Quote|We've ordered a blockade, curfew, and started a search operation. It's only a matter of time before we find the Rebels.|Unidentified Moradmin Bast look-alike to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="SWHS" />}}
A '''male Human''' served as an [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the early stages [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. [[Gender/Legends|He]] greatly resembled another Imperial, the [[General/Legends|General]] [[Moradmin Bast/Legends|Moradmin Bast]], and bore the same [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|ranking plaque]] as him.

In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], that [[Human/Legends|Human]] male worked under the direct command of the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. At the [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] [[Adjutant/Legends|adjutant]] participated in the hunt for the elusive [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]], which eventually led him to the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]—known to the Imperials as "Wookiee Planet C." The officer gave his men instructions for a [[Blockade of Kashyyyk (1 ABY)|planetary blockade]] and ordered them to conduct a house-to-house search for the Rebels. He subsequently gave [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader a progress report. The officer confidently assured [[Palpatine/Legends|the Emperor's]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] that their targets would soon be found and captured.<ref name="SWHS" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Still, the show has some interesting tidbits that are worth checking out. It's got a few deleted scenes from ''A New Hope'' featuring a Moradmin Bast lookalike (perhaps even his clone), it showed Kashyyyk for the first time, Boba Fett made his debut in a cartoon, and it featured another cantina scene.|Tim Veekhoven and Kevin Beentjes|<ref name="WhosWho">
{{SW|url=news/the-star-wars-holiday-special-cantina-we-may-not-thrive-friend-but-we-survive-friend|text=''The Star Wars Holiday Special'' Cantina: Who's Who|int=The Star Wars Holiday Special Cantina: Who's Who}}</ref>}}
Cut footage of [[Leslie Schofield]] as General Moradmin Bast in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] was reused for a scene in ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''. [[Leland Chee]] has noted that Chief Bast would not have had time to escape [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|the first Death Star]] before its destruction, meaning that the character in the ''Holiday Special'' is not Chief Bast.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?messageID=15725996#15725996|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-10-02|time=11:01 AM|quote=…someone pointed out to me that Bast is seen standing right next to Tarkin when he gives the order to fire when ready. Per the film, he's dead.}}</ref> In ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special Cantina: Who's Who]]'', an article written for the official [[StarWars.com]] blog, authors [[Tim Veekhoven]] and [[Kevin Beentjes]] theorized that the Bast lookalike may in fact have been a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of his.<ref name="WhosWho" />
